item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second the concerns about the name sounding very fake. It doesn't sound like a real name to the point of it not parsing as a name when I first read the Description, and I had to reread the opening a few times to realize "Star Villa" was a name, and you weren't saying "A Villa for Foundation Personnel, named Star". Starting out the description like that was a noticeable drain on readability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, I really enjoyed it, but a little more development would be nice before they go savin' everybody. It's not a Mary Sue situation, but I feel a little more exploration into oh no I'm literally nothing more than a communications network and the emotional crisis that would entail would be nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’ll give a more in depth reply later so check back for that but I used a name generator lmao so I wasn’t trying to be special snowflake I just chose the name from a list

I’ll reply to your other points when I get home from school

The point of him being good at it was to kinda show his loyalty to the foundation and want to not be useless if he was forced to sit around. Tanhony could probably describe it better than me, but yeah. I’m glad you took the time to share your thoughts. ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few things:

1) I don't mind the initial concept, unlucky Foundationer becomes one with the communication network, Gremlins 2-style. But, even with the power that that entails, they seem ridiculously good at managing and communicating and coordinating and helping and super-saving everybody. Were they that good at handling that kind of emergency management ahead of time? I don't know anything about this person, but man, I would think most regular people would be less likely to possess this kind of flawless skill at all, especially when they still seem like they possess the same personality they had before they became the Foundation's own Electric Gremlin. Plus, they sure seemed to hunker down and hide from everyone for a while too, before out of nowhere becoming an instantaneous information and communication master. Didn't ring true that the person in the interview was the same Crisis God shown over and over in the Prevention Log.

2) Honestly, the name "Star Villa" just threw me off and didn't sound like a real name. Even though it followed the phrase "Foundation Personnel" I still didn't catch that that was an actual name of a person because it was so weird. Sorry, that's probably more on me, but it sounds more like the name of a Caribbean beach cottage.

3) The epilogue again just pushed that This Person Is Just The Best Ever With No Negatives narrative even more in a very short period of time, and seems unearned for how quickly we get there.

Overall, everything seems way too easy for this skip, and everything always goes great, every time, which doesn't engender any sense of drama or impact, since it's just smooth sailing the whole way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Largely agreeing with this. The lack of risk is reflected in both the Thaumiel classification (like SCP-4818 is on Alpha-9, but she's still Euclid), as well as the incident log, which essentially boils down to "they save the day a bunch". The ending was cute, but because things were so "good" before, the impact wasn't as good as it could've been.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't do a lot for me. It's fairly short, the characters don't get much time to develop, and the events depicted are pretty straightforward. The even consistency of everything prevents the buildup of tension, and the bit that accumulates just before the security theater cutoff is immediately defused. Ideally, I would really want to see more at risk at some point throughout, either in terms of information security, the character's personal safety, or something else. As is, the article's calm doesn't feel tenuous at any point.

item.title: Co-Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Yo! I haven’t written something in a while aha. Anyway, Tanhony said it, this a collab between us! It was pretty fun to write. The idea was inspired by two of my abandoned drafts, one of the test logs is even a reference to one of them (the time loop one).

With that said thanks to those who crit it and I hope you enjoy!

item.title: Co-Author Post
item.content:encoded: Hi! This is a collab between Mew-ltiverse and myself! Thanks to zipzipskins, JakdragonX, DrAkimoto3 and Cregan_Stark for their feedback!
